segments.text: These are India's most popular brands.
But do you know they all are the copies of foreign brands. Each copy became bigger than the original one and one of them even crossed the line. So
paperboard's packaging mirrors retains Ella's kitchen juice pouches, identical fruit sketches and handdrawn fonts. But paperboard connected with India's childhood memories and sold 500 crores.
But you won't consider this copying when you see how Mahindra Tar copied Jeep's Wrangler iconic seven slot grill, round headlights and even boxy rugged design.
The THR is made for Indian roads and priced for Indian buyers. Selling over 200,000 units while the Wrangler remained a pricey import. But the best
one is dot and key. They lifted their entire look. Fruit droppers, paste labels, and even the bottles from Korea's Rudia and 100 crores in sales.
They copied every last detail. Subscribe for more amazing design content.
